DECISION 8 — Contingency Tracking Infrastructure (Founder Self-Report + Webhook, Not Invasive Polling): Create a Stripe invoice for each contingency fee on deal close (founder must claim it). Wire a Slack webhook (founder's private Slack channel) that sends a message on invoice creation: 'Contingency fee claimed — deal closed [date], ACV [amount], fee earned [5-10% calculation].' Accept founder email + Stripe invoice state as the canonical record; do NOT ask for receipts, LOIs, or legal proof (founders hate this). Rationale: Founders are on your side; trust them. The Slack message is your audit trail. If a dispute arises (founder says the deal closed in month 3, you have the Slack date + invoice timestamp). This keeps the relationship friendly while creating accountability.

DECISION 9 — Co-Founder Search Trigger (Non-Negotiable Timing): Do NOT hire co-founder or start recruiting until Week 7 and ONLY if (a) 5+ design partners signed + at least 1 contingency closure locked, (b) 3+ named case studies live, (c) founder testimonials recorded + social proof evident. Recruit for: (1) VP Security (hired to be CEO of the durable regulated-vertical bet), (2) VP Sales (hired to scale retainers + accelerator partnerships). Both start part-time (10-15 hrs/week) in Week 9-10 and ramp to full-time in Week 11+. Only move to Series Seed conversation if both are in place + excited about the vision. Rationale: Pre-PMF hiring is a graveyard. Prove the 8-week motion works first; the right co-founder will be EAGER to join a fast-moving team with paying customers + deal momentum.

DECISION 10 — Infrastructure Bridge to Durable Regulated Vertical (Locked in Week 7-8): The next phase is NOT another SaaS product; it's a regulated auditing firm (similar to Ankura, Crowe, or AICPA-accredited practices, but AI-focused). The 8-week product is the PROOF POINT + CASH ENGINE. In Week 7-8, document exactly which kolm modules plug into the durable bet: (a) src/keys.js + src/intoto-receipt.js = cryptographic chain-of-custody for attestations (regulatory-grade signature, not startup-scale) across many clients. (b) src/capture-store.js + src/trace-capture.js = append-only audit logs (SOC 2 Type II + ISO 42001 control evidence). (c) src/auth.js + src/router.js = tenant isolation + scoped access (multi-client auditing platform). (d) src/pii-redactor.js + src/gateway-router.js = redaction + evidence capture (regulatory-grade data handling). The durable bet is a PLATFORM that lets you audit 50+ AI startups simultaneously (multi-tenant, once-per-quarter re-attestation, lower marginal cost). Seed funding = $500K-$1.5M (covers hiring, legal/compliance, SEC audit, insurance, and 18-month runway). Series A = $10-30M when you've proven 5-10 regulated-vertical wins + $500K/yr ARR + proof the model scales. Rationale: selling attestations one-at-a-time is a consulting play; building the PLATFORM that every regulated AI startup uses is a venture-scale outcome.
open_questions: OPEN QUESTION 1 — Hiring + Equity (Week 7-8 Decision Point): Should the 5 design partners be offered equity in the eventual regulated-vertical company (Series Seed carve-out, e.g., 0.5-1% to early founder-partners)? Upside: founders become shareholders, more invested, better testimonials. Downside: dilution at Seed stage, legal complexity (option agreements, vesting), and may confuse the financial model if founders expect equity instead of cash retainer. RECOMMENDED: Offer no equity during 8-week sprint. At Week 8, IF 3+ design partners want to go deeper (become advisors or board observers in the durable bet), offer 0.5% options at Series Seed strike price. Keeps Week 1-8 clean; doesn't block deals.

OPEN QUESTION 2 — Regulated Jurisdiction + Legal Entity (Week 7-8 Planning): Where will the durable regulated-auditing firm be incorporated? U.S. federal (Delaware + AICPA path to CPE/peer review)? EU (German/Irish for GDPR credibility on EU AI Act auditing)? Singapore (emerging AI-regulation hub, lower upfront compliance cost)? RECOMMENDED: Incorporate in Delaware for Seed funding (VCs default to DE); add a UK/EU subsidiary in Year 2 if you land EU AI Act auditing work. The 8-week sprint is U.S.-focused (YC + Techstars founders are mostly U.S.); expand regionally after Seed closes.

OPEN QUESTION 3 — Insurance + Professional Liability (Critical Before Week 3 Launch): What professional liability and errors-and-omissions (E&O) insurance is required to sign attestations? AICPA-accredited auditors carry $2-5M E&O policies. Do you need this for the 8-week sprint? RECOMMENDED: (a) For Week 1-8, operate under a 'Limited Audit Attestation' scope (NOT full SOC2, NOT full audit opinion). Insurance requirement: $250K-$1M E&O policy (cost ~$5-10K/year). (b) Explicitly disclaim 'this is not an audit opinion' in every report. (c) Move to AICPA accreditation + $3M+ E&O policy when you move to the durable regulated bet (Series Seed). Consult a compliance lawyer (cost ~$3-5K) in Week 1 to draft the insurance+liability language.

OPEN QUESTION 4 — Contingency Fee Enforceability (Legal Dependency): Is a 5-10% contingency fee enforceable if the founder's sales VP actually closed the deal and the founder claims 'the audit didn't help'? RECOMMENDED: Lawyer review (same $3-5K consultation) to draft a contract that: (a) requires the founder to confirm in writing 'deal was stuck in security review; audit was material to close.' (b) ties the fee to a specific Stripe invoice + close-date confirmation email (so you have paper trail). (c) caps total contingency liability at the deal ACV (so you don't accidentally owe 10% of a deal worth more than you thought). (d) includes a 'disputes resolved via expedited binding arbitration, not litigation' clause (keeps disputes cheap). No contract makes contingency bulletproof; lawyer review at least reduces surprises.

OPEN QUESTION 5 — Attestation Liability + Scope Creep (Ongoing Risk): If a founder's agent causes a security incident AFTER you've signed an attestation saying the agent's permissions are 'correct,' can the founder's buyer sue you? RECOMMENDED: (a) Insurance + liability cap in the contract: 'Auditor's total liability is capped at the fee paid ($25K). This attestation is a point-in-time assessment; changes to the agent's config, model, or deployment after the audit date may invalidate findings.' (b) Explicitly scope to 'API/permission posture + audit-trail integrity; prompt-injection defense is NOT comprehensive.' (c) Add a 're-audit trigger clause: 'If the startup changes agents, adds new tools, or deploys to a new runtime, they must re-attest (recommended quarterly).' This converts liability risk into retainer revenue. (d) Hire an insurance broker (cost ~$1-2K fee) in Week 1 to design an insurance + contract framework before you sign the first design-partner deal.

OPEN QUESTION 6 — Red-Team Subcontracting (Cost & Execution Model): Who executes the PyRIT/Garak testing? (a) You (build + run the harness yourself)? (b) Subcontract to Prompt Security / Robust Intelligence / EvalAI (cost $2-5K per engagement)? (c) Mix: run simple ASI01-02 in-house, subcontract ASI03-10 to a specialist? RECOMMENDED: Mix model. In-house (Week 1): build a PyRIT/Garak wrapper that runs against the startup's model endpoint + captures 20-30 injection attempts. If it fails any, report it + stop (pass/fail gate). If founder wants deeper red-teaming, refer to HackerOne + take a 10% referral if they book through you. Rationale: bounds your liability, maintains schedule, and creates a revenue backfill (referral fees).

OPEN QUESTION 7 — Report Template + PDF Generation (Build vs. Buy Decision): Should you build the Markdown → PDF pipeline yourself (cost ~80 hrs) or buy a template service (Typst, Pandoc + custom CSS, or Descript)? RECOMMENDED: Use Typst (open-source, deterministic PDF generation, great for technical documents, ~40 hrs to build custom template). Avoid closed commercial services (Figma, Canva) because you need the source template + signature block to be repeatable + version-controlled. Typst source lives in Git; every report is generated from the same template, so findings are isolated from design changes.

OPEN QUESTION 8 — Regulatory Updates + Report Versioning (Cadence & Communication): How often do you re-baseline the report template when NIST / EU AI Act / OWASP guidance changes? RECOMMENDED: Quarterly sync (Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct) with NIST AI RMF, EU AI Office, and OWASP releases. If a material change occurs (e.g., EU enforcement cliff Aug 2 2026), send a 'Report Update Alert' to all current retainers: 'Your attestation is valid but affected by [framework change]. We recommend a delta re-assessment for $3K (vs. full re-audit at $25K).' This keeps founders engaged + creates recurring revenue without requiring a full re-audit.

OPEN QUESTION 9 — Accelerator Partnerships (Pilot vs. Rollout): Do you lock a formal partnership with YC (or Techstars) in Week 1-2, or do you pilot with individual founders first + formalize later? RECOMMENDED: Pilot first (Weeks 1-4: land 2-3 design partners via warm intros to YC founders you know). THEN approach YC's security lead (Ali Yahya or founder community lead) in Week 5 with proof ('3 YC founders have used the audit, here are case studies'). Propose: 'We offer 25% discount to YC companies; you feature in security office hours quarterly.' Formal partnership = LOI + no exclusivity (so you can also work with Techstars + founders outside YC). Rationale: YC is easier to land WITH proof-of-concept than as your first move.

OPEN QUESTION 10 — Eventual Regulated-Vertical Bet (Sector Choice): Healthcare SaMD (FDA + ISO 62304), Finance AI (SEC + OCC), EU High-Risk (EU AI Act + national regs)? RECOMMENDED DEFER this decision to Week 7-8 (after design partners give you signals). Ask each design partner: 'If we built a continuous audit platform, what would make it valuable to your enterprise buyers?' Their answers guide the vertical. If 3+ say 'healthcare,' that's a signal. If 2+ say 'EU compliance,' pursue that. Do NOT pick the vertical based on market-size TAM slide; pick it based on where your founders are already selling + where the regulatory cliff creates urgency (EU AI Act Aug 2 2026 is now your biggest tailwind).

OPEN QUESTION 11 — Marketing & Content Strategy (Brand Positioning Refinement): Should the website messaging emphasize the 'deal unblocking' angle or the 'compliance expertise' angle? RECOMMENDED: Lead with deal unblocking ('From 8 weeks to 10 days') for founder acquisition; back up with compliance expertise (framework mapping, signed attestations) for enterprise buyer acceptance. Homepage headline: 'Unblock Your Enterprise Security Review in 2 Weeks.' Subheader: 'Cryptographically signed attestation for AI agents — mapped to SOC2, NIST, EU AI Act, OWASP.' Navigation CTA: 'Schedule a pilot' (not 'request a demo'; founders hate that language). By Week 4, you should have 2-3 case studies live + 1-2 video testimonials recorded so new founders see proof this works.
